column1: Yes, I know, big boy, says Mama Bimbo. "I was hopin' you'd come next door!"
column2: 「是啊，我知道，大帥哥，」賓波媽媽說。「人家還盼著你會過來隔壁咧！」

column1: There's nothing in there that you know how to use!
column2: 那裡面沒有你會用的東西！

column1: Well, well, well; what have we here? says the woman on the bed.
column2: 「哎呀哎呀哎呀，這是什麼呀？」床上的女人說。

column1: Uh, oh, Larry. You've been caught entering someone else's stateroom.
column2: 糟了，賴瑞，你闖進別人房間被逮個正著。

column1: Why, I do believe it's that darling young man that my daughter won on TV! says the woman.
column2: 「哎呀，這不就是我女兒在電視上贏來的那個小可愛嗎！」女人說。

column1: Good lord! She's Bachelorette Barbara's mother!
column2: 天啊！她是單身女郎芭芭拉的媽媽！

column1: Poor little Barbie got to feeling poorly just as the cruise was about to start, so I decided to 'borrow' her ticket and come along instead!
column2: 「可憐的小芭比在遊輪要出發時剛好身體不舒服，所以我就決定『借』她的票，自己來啦！」

column1: Your mind quickly envisions "Paradise Lost."
column2: 你腦中瞬間浮現「失樂園」的畫面。

column1: I hope you don't mind: I convinced the purser that it was in his best interests to assign us to adjoining cabins! I'm sure I can make your voyage enjoyable!
column2: 「希望你別介意：我說服事務長，讓我們住相鄰的艙房對他比較有利！我保證能讓你這趟航程過得愉快！」

column1: I really want to get to 'know' you (if you get my drift). After I saw you win that Lottery show, I thought you were just about the cutest thing I'd ever seen!
column2: 「人家真的很想好好『認識』你（你懂我的意思吧）。自從看到你在那個樂透節目贏獎之後，我就覺得你是我見過最可愛的小東西！」

column1: Except for my Mister, of course (may he rest in peace). He was a good man, and usually quite sturdy, but one day his heart just couldn't take it any more!
column2: 「當然啦，我家那口子除外（願他安息）。他是個好人，身子骨通常也挺硬朗，可是有一天他的心臟就是撐不住了！」

column1: You wonder why it is necessary for you to learn this.
column2: 你搞不懂為什麼有必要知道這件事。

column1: Honey, any time you want to come over and see Momma, you just feel free to pop on through that doorway, ya hear?
column2: 「甜心，你什麼時候想來看媽咪，儘管從那扇門過來，聽到沒？」

column1: She attempts to give you a cute, girlish smile.
column2: 她試著對你露出一個可愛的少女式微笑。

column1: (She fails.)
column2: （失敗了。）

column1: Say, look who's back! says the Mother. "It's my little friend from next door! Came over for a little action, did we? Well, you've come to the right place, big boy!"
column2: 「哎呀，看看誰回來啦！」媽媽說。「是我隔壁的小朋友嘛！是來找點刺激的吧？嘿，你可找對地方啦，大帥哥！」

column1: You've been caught in someone else's stateroom!
column2: 你闖進別人房間被逮到了！

column1: You decide to make a run for the door!
column2: 你決定拔腿衝向門口！

column1: Yep, I've been lookin' for a man like you ever since my Mister had his big one. Whaddaya say we have a little fun? You do like havin' fun, doncha?
column2: 「沒錯，自從我家那口子『掛』了以後，我就一直在找像你這樣的男人。咱們來找點樂子怎麼樣？你喜歡玩樂吧，對不對？」

column1: Hop over there on the bed, baby.
column2: 「快上床來吧，寶貝。」

column1: Now you just get comfortable in those chromium chains and handcuffs while I slip into something a little more comfortable myself, she says, opening her closet. "Let's see, possibly a little something in mink-lined leather would be nice..."
column2: 「你就舒舒服服地戴上那副鍍鉻鎖鍊和手銬吧，我也去換件更輕鬆的衣服，」她一邊說一邊打開衣櫥。「讓我瞧瞧……來件貂皮內裡的皮衣應該不錯……」

column1: Mama proceeds to have her way with you repeatedly.
column2: 媽媽一次又一次地對你為所欲為。

column1: Too bad that "her way" is not "your way!"
column2: 可惜「她的玩法」不是「你的玩法」！

column1: (You can see why this broad's a widow!)
column2: （你終於明白這女人為什麼會守寡了！）

column1: Hi, sweetie! says Mama.
column2: 「嗨，甜心！」媽媽說。

column1: Back to play?
column2: 「回來找樂子啦？」

column1: You find women sexy.
column2: 你覺得女人很性感。

column1: (But in this case, you make an exception!)
column2: （不過這次你破例了！）

column1: You allow yourself to be seduced by the beautiful woman and quickly become close friends, then head for the ship's fantail where she parks her private helicopter. A short, romantic chopper ride across the South Pacific is followed by an intriguing look at her impressive garage door, hidden suspiciously by a mountain waterfall.
column2: 你讓自己被這位美麗的女人迷惑，兩人很快就打得火熱，接著前往船尾甲板，她把私人直升機停在那兒。一段浪漫的南太平洋直升機之旅後，你們降落，一探她那扇引人遐想的車庫門，那扇門居然巧妙地藏在一座山間瀑布後面。

column1: Later, that night...
column2: 當天晚上稍後……

column1: Hey, baby, you cry, "wait for me!!"
column2: 「嘿，寶貝，」你大喊，「等等我！！」

column1: They are all too busy sunbathing to talk to you.
column2: 她們都忙著做日光浴，沒空理你。

column1: There's only one deck chair left.
column2: 只剩下一張甲板椅了。

column1: The webbing has stretched considerably since you plopped down!
column2: 自從你一屁股坐下去之後，那張帆布椅已經被撐得鬆垮垮的了！

column1: Just think how ugly he must look from underneath!
column2: 想想看從底下看他一定有多醜！

column1: Your botanical investigation leads to nothing.
column2: 你的植物學調查毫無所獲。

column1: Everything around you looks wet.
column2: 你周遭的一切看起來都濕答答的。

column1: You're too busy watching your life pass before your eyes!
column2: 你正忙著看自己的一生在眼前跑馬燈呢！

column1: You look straight up and see lots of blue sky.
column2: 你抬頭直望，看到一大片藍天。

column1: Doesn't that pool look refreshing?
column2: 那座泳池看起來是不是很消暑？

column1: There is one empty chaise lounge by the pool.
column2: 泳池旁有一張空的躺椅。

column1: You are!
column2: 你就是啊！

column1: Good idea, Larry.
column2: 好主意，賴瑞。

column1: Try stepping into the pool first!
column2: 先試著踏進泳池吧！

column1: You can't. You're too busy drowning!
column2: 你辦不到，你正忙著溺水呢！

column1: You try repeatedly to dive into the concrete pool deck with no success!
column2: 你一再嘗試往水泥泳池甲板跳水，全都以失敗收場！

column1: Not here! You might hit the edge of the pool.
column2: 別在這裡跳！你可能會撞到池邊。

column1: This is too comfortable for you to change positions now!
column2: 這姿勢舒服到你現在懶得換了！

column1: You're not properly dressed for sunbathing; where's your swimming suit?
column2: 你這身打扮不適合做日光浴啊，你的泳褲呢？

column1: Not now! There's someone coming.
column2: 現在不行！有人來了。

column1: Nah. That's unnecessary.
column2: 算了，沒這個必要。

column1: Hi ya, beautiful, you tell her. %s
column2: 「嗨，美女，」你對她說。%s

column1: To whom are you speaking? There are no girls here.
column2: 你在跟誰說話啊？這裡根本沒有女生。

column1: You have always loved bathing suits with the consistency of facial tissue!
column2: 你一直很喜歡那種薄得跟面紙一樣的泳裝！

column1: Where? There's nothing here but guys.
column2: 哪裡？這裡只有一堆男的。

column1: Next time, you might try swimming in something other than a thick polyester white leisure suit!
column2: 下次也許可以試著別穿著厚重的白色聚酯纖維休閒西裝去游泳！

column1: Why jump in the water if you're not going to swim?
column2: 不游泳幹嘛跳進水裡？

column1: Surely you learned to swim when you were a little dorker?
column2: 你小時候當個小呆瓜的時候，總該學過游泳吧？

column1: That was refreshing!
column2: 真是暢快啊！

column1: Ah! Seems like a lovely day to catch a few rays.
column2: 啊！今天真是曬曬太陽的好天氣。

column1: It's a good thing you applied that sunscreen!
column2: 還好你有擦防曬乳！

column1: Those tropical UVs can be deadly!
column2: 熱帶的紫外線可是要人命的！

column1: Why, hello there, big fella, says the beautiful blond with the body like a brick shipyard, "why are you hanging around here? I've been looking for someone just like you for a long time. Why don't you come back to my place, and you won't have to hang around all alone ever again!"
column2: 「哎呀，你好啊，大帥哥，」那位身材火辣、像座磚砌船塢一樣結實的金髮美女說，「你怎麼一個人晃來晃去？我找像你這樣的人找好久了。要不要跟我回家，這樣你就不用再自己一個人晃了！」

column1: (Geez, Larry. You've finally found yourself a live one!)
column2: （天啊，賴瑞，你終於釣到一條活魚了！）

column1: Come on, Samson, I just can't wait to get you all alone!
column2: 「來吧，參孫，人家等不及要把你一個人佔為己有了！」

column1: You meet the lovely blond on the ship's stairs, quickly become dear friends, then head for the fantail where she parks her private helicopter. A romantic, moonlight chopper ride across the South Pacific is followed by an intriguing look at her impressive garage door, which looks suspiciously like a mountaintop waterfall.
column2: 你在船上的階梯遇見這位可愛的金髮女郎，兩人很快就成了知心好友，接著前往船尾甲板，她把私人直升機停在那兒。一段月光下橫越南太平洋的浪漫直升機之旅後，你們一探她那扇引人遐想的車庫門，那扇門看起來活像座山頂瀑布，藏得可疑極了。

column1: Hey, baby, you cry, "wait for me! I think I love ya!"
column2: 「嘿，寶貝，」你大喊，「等等我！我想我愛上你了！」

column1: Hi ya, beautiful, you tell her. %s Where'dja get that dress?
column2: 「嗨，美女，」你對她說。%s 你這件洋裝哪買的？

column1: Too bad she couldn't afford to buy a complete dress!
column2: 可惜她沒錢買件完整的洋裝！

column1: She looks like she has something you need, Larry.
column2: 賴瑞，她身上好像有你需要的東西。

column1: (And vice versa.)
column2: （反之亦然。）

column1: A beautiful woman is entering the bar, and it looks like she's heading for you, Larry
column2: 一位美女走進酒吧，看起來是朝你走過來的，賴瑞

column1: BOING!
column2: 咚！

column1: He looks extremely bored.
column2: 他看起來無聊透頂。

column1: All of the tables are occupied by people enjoying themselves.
column2: 所有的桌子都被玩得很開心的人佔滿了。

column1: (And they don't want to be bothered by you!)
column2: （而且他們也不想被你打擾！）

column1: There's something moving out there!
column2: 外面有東西在動！

column1: Oh, wait. That's just the ship; this room is revolving!
column2: 喔，等等，那只是船而已，這房間本身在旋轉！

column1: You've always loved black lights.
column2: 你一直很喜歡黑燈。

column1: Shouldn't you turn off this computer if you're going to watch television?
column2: 你要看電視的話，不是該先把這台電腦關掉嗎？

column1: The bartender is looking at you, expecting you to order a drink.
column2: 酒保正看著你，等你點杯酒。

column1: There is a handsome bartender behind the bar and one empty stool in front of it.
column2: 吧台後面有位帥氣的酒保，吧台前有一張空的高腳椅。

column1: There is a complimentary bowl of spinach dip at the left end of the bar.
column2: 吧台左邊擺著一碗招待的菠菜沾醬。

column1: The spinach dip resides in a bowl made of sourdough bread, and looks delicious.
column2: 這碗菠菜沾醬盛在酸種麵包做的碗裡，看起來很美味。

column1: The ship's revolving nightclub has a television set behind the bar and only one empty stool.
column2: 這間船上的旋轉夜店，吧台後面有台電視，只剩一張空的高腳椅。

column1: There's only one seat available to you, Larry: that empty stool at the bar.
column2: 賴瑞，你只有一個位子可以坐：吧台那張空的高腳椅。

column1: Hi, you say to the bartender with a smile. %s
column2: 「嗨，」你微笑著對酒保說。%s

column1: Thanks for telling me, he responds sarcastically.
column2: 「謝謝你告訴我啊，」他語帶諷刺地回答。

column1: (Well, that didn't work Larry!)
column2: （嗯，賴瑞，這招沒用啊！）

column1: Hi, you bevy of beautiful babes, you say to the women at the bar, flashing your best smile. %s
column2: 「嗨，各位美女們，」你對吧台的女生們說，露出你最帥的笑容。%s

column1: Why don't you join hands and try communicating with the living? she asks sarcastically.
column2: 「你要不要找人牽手來跟活人溝通看看啊？」她語帶諷刺地問。

column1: What an asshole, she tells her friend.
column2: 「真是個混蛋，」她跟朋友說。

column1: There's no one here interested in talking to you. Perhaps you could talk to the bartender.
column2: 這裡沒有人有興趣跟你講話，也許你可以找酒保聊聊。

column1: We're all out of that, says the bartender. "Would you like a mixed drink?"
column2: 「那個賣完了，」酒保說。「要不要來杯調酒？」

column1: Ok. You dip a little sourdough bread into the mayonnaise-rich dip. Say! This is tasty. You ravenously devour the entire bowl, consuming approximately a quart of mayonnaise in the process and blowing your diet for the next month. Geez, Larry; you could have left a little for the other people here!
column2: 好的。你抓了一點酸種麵包沾了點美乃滋味十足的沾醬。哇！真好吃。你狼吞虎嚥地把整碗都吃光了，過程中大概吃下一夸脫的美乃滋，把接下來一個月的減肥計畫都毀了。天啊，賴瑞，你好歹也留一點給其他人吧！

column1: You grab the loaf of french bread filled with spinach, mayonnaise and spices. Umm, good!
column2: 你抓起那條裡面塞滿菠菜、美乃滋和香料的法國麵包。嗯，好吃！

column1: Do you have any drinks with parasols and fruit? you ask the bartender.
column2: 「你們有沒有插著小紙傘、加水果的那種飲料？」你問酒保。

column1: But, of course, he replies, "allow me to fix you the speciality of the house..."
column2: 「那當然，」他回答，「讓我為你調一杯本店招牌……」

column1: Heavy on the pineapple, ok? you ask.
column2: 「鳳梨多加一點，好嗎？」你問。

column1: No problem, he says. "Somehow, I would have guessed that!"
column2: 「沒問題，」他說。「不知怎的，我就猜到你會這麼說！」
